title: raphanistrum raphanistrum
intro: Raphanistrum raphanistrum, also known as wild celery or devil's thimble, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Raphanistrum raphanistrum include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate menstrual cramps. Raphanistrum raphanistrum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
